grep.h(3) Library Functions Manual grep.h(3)

NAME

grep.h

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct grep_parse_result_t
Parsed pattern result from /pattern/flags syntax.

Functions


grep_parse_result_t grep_parse_pattern (const char *input)
Parse pattern in /pattern/flags format or plain regex format. asciichat_error_t grep_init (const char *pattern)
Initialize log filtering with PCRE2 regex pattern. bool grep_should_output (const char *log_line, size_t *match_start, size_t *match_len)
Check if log line should be output (matches filter). const char * grep_highlight (const char *log_line, size_t match_start, size_t match_len)
Highlight matched portion with background color. const char * grep_highlight_colored (const char *colored_text, const char *plain_text, size_t match_start, size_t match_len)
Highlight matches in colored text while preserving original colors. char * grep_highlight_colored_copy (const char *colored_text, const char *plain_text, size_t match_start, size_t match_len)
Highlight matches in colored text, returning a new allocated copy. void grep_destroy (void)
Clean up filter resources. asciichat_error_t grep_save_patterns (void)
Save current filter patterns for later restoration. asciichat_error_t grep_restore_patterns (void)
Restore previously saved filter patterns. void grep_clear_patterns (void)
Clear all filter patterns. int grep_get_pattern_count (void)
Get number of active filter patterns. const char * grep_get_last_pattern (void)
Get the last (most recent) filter pattern string.

Function Documentation

void grep_clear_patterns (void )

Clear all filter patterns. Temporarily disables filtering by setting pattern count to 0. Patterns remain allocated and can be restored later. Used by interactive grep when user types first character.

Definition at line 1303 of file grep.c.

1303                                {
1304   // Don't free patterns, just set count to 0
1305   // This allows quick restore without reallocation
1306   g_filter_state.pattern_count = 0;
1307   g_filter_state.enabled = false;
1308 }








void
  grep_destroy (void )


Clean up filter resources. Frees compiled regex. Thread-local
    match_data is cleaned up via pthread_key destructor.


Definition at line 1165 of file grep.c.


1165                         {
1166   // Free all patterns
1167   for (int i = 0; i < g_filter_state.pattern_count; i++) {
1168     grep_pattern_t *pat = &g_filter_state.patterns[i];
1169     // Free the copied original string (need temp variable due to const qualifier)
1170     char *original_to_free = (char *)pat->original;
1171     SAFE_FREE(original_to_free);
1172     SAFE_FREE(pat->parsed_pattern);
1173     // Singletons are auto-cleaned by asciichat_pcre2_cleanup_all()
1174     pat->singleton = NULL;
1175   }
1176   SAFE_FREE(g_filter_state.patterns);
1177   g_filter_state.patterns = NULL; // Make idempotent
1178 
1179   // Free context buffer
1180   if (g_filter_state.line_buffer) {
1181     for (int i = 0; i < g_filter_state.buffer_size; i++) {
1182       SAFE_FREE(g_filter_state.line_buffer[i]);
1183     }
1184     SAFE_FREE(g_filter_state.line_buffer);
1185     g_filter_state.line_buffer = NULL; // Make idempotent
1186   }
1187 
1188   g_filter_state.pattern_count = 0;
1189   g_filter_state.pattern_capacity = 0;
1190   g_filter_state.buffer_size = 0;
1191   g_filter_state.buffer_pos = 0;
1192   g_filter_state.lines_after_match = 0;
1193   g_filter_state.max_context_after = 0;
1194   g_filter_state.enabled = false;
1195 
1196   // Note: Thread-local match_data is cleaned up via pthread_key destructor
1197 }






References grep_pattern_t::original,
    grep_pattern_t::parsed_pattern, SAFE_FREE, and
    grep_pattern_t::singleton.


Referenced by log_destroy().






const
  char * grep_get_last_pattern (void )


Get the last (most recent) filter pattern string. Returns the
    original pattern string (with /pattern/flags syntax if provided) of the last
    --grep argument. Used by interactive grep to populate its input buffer when
    starting with CLI --grep patterns.




Returns


Pattern string of last CLI --grep, or NULL if no
  patterns


Definition at line 1314 of file grep.c.


1314                                         {
1315   if (g_filter_state.pattern_count == 0) {
1316     return NULL;
1317   }
1318 
1319   // Return the original pattern string of the last pattern
1320   return g_filter_state.patterns[g_filter_state.pattern_count - 1].original;
1321 }






Referenced by log_search_enter_mode(),
    log_search_init(), ui_status_display(), and
    ui_status_display_interactive().






int
  grep_get_pattern_count (void )


Get number of active filter patterns.


Returns


Number of currently active patterns


Definition at line 1310 of file grep.c.


1310                                  {
1311   return g_filter_state.pattern_count;
1312 }










const
  char * grep_highlight (const char * log_line, size_t match_start, size_t
  match_len)


Highlight matched portion with background color. Inserts ANSI
    escape codes for yellow background (255, 200, 0) with black foreground (0,
    0, 0) around the matched text.




Parameters


log_line Original log line


match_start Start offset of match


match_len Length of match


Returns


Highlighted string (pointer to static thread-local
  buffer)


Definition at line 1062 of file grep.c.


1062                                                                                        {
1063   static __thread char highlight_buffer[16384];
1064 
1065   if (!log_line || match_len == 0) {
1066     return log_line; // No highlighting needed
1067   }
1068 
1069   size_t line_len = strlen(log_line);
1070   if (match_start + match_len > line_len) {
1071     return log_line; // Invalid range
1072   }
1073 
1074   char *dst = highlight_buffer;
1075   const char *src = log_line;
1076   size_t pos = 0;
1077 
1078   // If any pattern has global flag, highlight ALL matches
1079   grep_pattern_t *global_pat = NULL;
1080   for (int i = 0; i < g_filter_state.pattern_count; i++) {
1081     if (g_filter_state.patterns[i].global_flag && !g_filter_state.patterns[i].is_fixed_string) {
1082       global_pat = &g_filter_state.patterns[i];
1083       break;
1084     }
1085   }
1086 
1087   if (global_pat && global_pat->singleton) {
1088     pcre2_code *code = asciichat_pcre2_singleton_get_code(global_pat->singleton);
1089     pcre2_match_data *match_data = get_thread_match_data();
1090 
1091     if (!code || !match_data) {
1092       return log_line; // Fall back to no highlighting
1093     }
1094 
1095     // Find all matches in the line
1096     size_t offset = 0;
1097     while (offset < line_len) {
1098       int rc = pcre2_jit_match(code, (PCRE2_SPTR)log_line, line_len, offset, 0, match_data, NULL);
1099       if (rc < 0) {
1100         break; // No more matches
1101       }
1102 
1103       PCRE2_SIZE *ovector = pcre2_get_ovector_pointer(match_data);
1104       size_t match_pos = (size_t)ovector[0];
1105       size_t match_end = (size_t)ovector[1];
1106       size_t len = match_end - match_pos;
1107 
1108       // Copy text before match
1109       if (match_pos > pos) {
1110         memcpy(dst, src + pos, match_pos - pos);
1111         dst += (match_pos - pos);
1112       }
1113 
1114       // Highlight the match (background only, preserve foreground color)
1115       uint8_t r, g, b;
1116       get_highlight_color(&r, &g, &b);
1117       dst = append_truecolor_bg(dst, r, g, b);
1118       memcpy(dst, src + match_pos, len);
1119       dst += len;
1120 
1121       pos = match_end;
1122       offset = match_end;
1123 
1124       // Prevent infinite loop on zero-length matches
1125       if (len == 0) {
1126         offset++;
1127       }
1128     }
1129 
1130     // Copy remaining text after last match
1131     if (pos < line_len) {
1132       memcpy(dst, src + pos, line_len - pos);
1133       dst += (line_len - pos);
1134     }
1135 
1136   } else {
1137     // Single match highlighting (original behavior)
1138     // Copy text before match
1139     if (match_start > 0) {
1140       memcpy(dst, log_line, match_start);
1141       dst += match_start;
1142     }
1143 
1144     // Add background color for match (preserve foreground color)
1145     uint8_t r, g, b;
1146     get_highlight_color(&r, &g, &b);
1147     dst = append_truecolor_bg(dst, r, g, b);
1148 
1149     // Copy matched text
1150     memcpy(dst, log_line + match_start, match_len);
1151     dst += match_len;
1152 
1153     // Copy text after match
1154     size_t remaining = line_len - (match_start + match_len);
1155     if (remaining > 0) {
1156       memcpy(dst, log_line + match_start + match_len, remaining);
1157       dst += remaining;
1158     }
1159   }
1160 
1161   *dst = '\0';
1162   return highlight_buffer;
1163 }






References append_truecolor_bg(),
    asciichat_pcre2_singleton_get_code(), and
    grep_pattern_t::singleton.






const
  char * grep_highlight_colored (const char * colored_text, const char *
  plain_text, size_t match_start, size_t match_len)


Highlight matches in colored text while preserving original
    colors.


Parameters


colored_text Original text with ANSI color codes


plain_text Stripped plain text (for match positions)


match_start Match start position in plain text


match_len Match length in plain text


Returns


Highlighted text with yellow background added to matches
  (pointer to static thread-local buffer)


Definition at line 743 of file grep.c.


744                                                      {
745   static __thread char highlight_buffer[16384];
746 
747   if (!colored_text || !plain_text || match_len == 0) {
748     return colored_text;
749   }
750 
751   // When interactive grep is active, check if it has global flag
752   bool is_log_search = log_search_is_active();
753   bool should_use_global_pattern = false;
754   grep_pattern_t *global_pat = NULL;
755 
756   if (!is_log_search) {
757     // If any pattern has global flag, highlight ALL matches for that pattern
758     // Find first pattern with global flag
759     for (int i = 0; i < g_filter_state.pattern_count; i++) {
760       if (g_filter_state.patterns[i].global_flag && !g_filter_state.patterns[i].is_fixed_string) {
761         global_pat = &g_filter_state.patterns[i];
762         should_use_global_pattern = true;
763         break;
764       }
765     }
766   } else if (log_search_get_global_highlight()) {
767     // Interactive grep is active and has /g flag - do global matching on the provided pattern
768     // We'll do all-match highlighting using the match position to extract the pattern
769     // and re-match for all occurrences
770     should_use_global_pattern = true;
771     // global_pat stays NULL, but we set the flag to enable global matching below
772   }
773 
774   if (should_use_global_pattern) {
775     pcre2_code *code = NULL;
776     pcre2_match_data *match_data = NULL;
777     char fixed_string_pattern[256] = {0};
778     bool is_fixed_string_pattern = false;
779     bool case_insensitive_fixed = false;
780 
781     // Get code from CLI pattern or interactive grep pattern
782     if (global_pat && global_pat->singleton) {
783       code = asciichat_pcre2_singleton_get_code(global_pat->singleton);
784       match_data = get_thread_match_data();
785     } else if (is_log_search) {
786       // Interactive grep global highlighting - get pattern from grep state
787       void *grep_singleton_void = log_search_get_pattern_singleton();
788       if (grep_singleton_void) {
789         pcre2_singleton_t *grep_singleton = (pcre2_singleton_t *)grep_singleton_void;
790         code = asciichat_pcre2_singleton_get_code(grep_singleton);
791         // Create match data for this pattern
792         if (code) {
793           match_data = pcre2_match_data_create_from_pattern(code, NULL);
794         }
795       } else {
796         // No regex pattern - check if it's a fixed string pattern in interactive grep
797         int pattern_len = log_search_get_input_len();
798         const char *pattern = log_search_get_input_buffer();
799         if (pattern_len > 0 && pattern && pattern_len < (int)sizeof(fixed_string_pattern)) {
800           SAFE_STRNCPY(fixed_string_pattern, pattern, sizeof(fixed_string_pattern) - 1);
801           is_fixed_string_pattern = true;
802           case_insensitive_fixed = log_search_get_case_insensitive();
803         }
804       }
805     }
806 
807     if (!code && !is_fixed_string_pattern) {
808       if (match_data && is_log_search) {
809         pcre2_match_data_free(match_data); // Free if we created it
810       }
811       return colored_text; // Fall back to no highlighting
812     }
813 
814     size_t plain_len = strlen(plain_text);
815     size_t colored_len = strlen(colored_text);
816     char *dst = highlight_buffer;
817     size_t plain_offset = 0;
818     size_t colored_pos = 0;
819 
820     // Handle fixed string global matching
821     if (is_fixed_string_pattern) {
822       while (plain_offset < plain_len) {
823         // Find next occurrence of fixed string
824         const char *found = NULL;
825         if (case_insensitive_fixed) {
826           found = utf8_strcasestr(plain_text + plain_offset, fixed_string_pattern);
827         } else {
828           found = strstr(plain_text + plain_offset, fixed_string_pattern);
829         }
830 
831         if (!found) {
832           break; // No more matches
833         }
834 
835         size_t plain_match_start = (size_t)(found - plain_text);
836         size_t plain_match_end = plain_match_start + strlen(fixed_string_pattern);
837 
838         // Map to colored text positions (same as single-match path)
839         size_t colored_match_start = map_plain_to_colored_pos(colored_text, plain_match_start);
840         size_t colored_match_end = map_plain_to_colored_pos(colored_text, plain_match_end);
841 
842         // Copy text before match
843         if (colored_match_start > colored_pos) {
844           memcpy(dst, colored_text + colored_pos, colored_match_start - colored_pos);
845           dst += (colored_match_start - colored_pos);
846         }
847 
848         // Add highlight background
849         uint8_t r, g, b;
850         get_highlight_color(&r, &g, &b);
851         dst = append_truecolor_bg(dst, r, g, b);
852 
853         // Copy matched text with background
854         size_t match_byte_len = colored_match_end - colored_match_start;
855         memcpy(dst, colored_text + colored_match_start, match_byte_len);
856         dst += match_byte_len;
857 
858         // Reset background
859         memcpy(dst, "\x1b[49m", 5);
860         dst += 5;
861 
862         colored_pos = colored_match_end;
863         plain_offset = plain_match_end;
864       }
865 
866       // Copy remaining text
867       if (colored_pos < colored_len) {
868         memcpy(dst, colored_text + colored_pos, colored_len - colored_pos);
869         dst += (colored_len - colored_pos);
870       }
871 
872       // Reset both background and foreground at end
873       *dst = '\0';
874       return highlight_buffer;
875     }
876 
877     // Find all matches in plain text and highlight in colored text (regex matching)
878     while (plain_offset < plain_len) {
879       int rc = pcre2_jit_match(code, (PCRE2_SPTR)plain_text, plain_len, plain_offset, 0, match_data, NULL);
880       if (rc < 0) {
881         break; // No more matches
882       }
883 
884       PCRE2_SIZE *ovector = pcre2_get_ovector_pointer(match_data);
885       size_t plain_match_start = (size_t)ovector[0];
886       size_t plain_match_end = (size_t)ovector[1];
887 
888       // Map to colored text positions
889       size_t colored_match_start = map_plain_to_colored_pos(colored_text, plain_match_start);
890       size_t colored_match_end = map_plain_to_colored_pos(colored_text, plain_match_end);
891 
892       // Copy text before match
893       if (colored_match_start > colored_pos) {
894         memcpy(dst, colored_text + colored_pos, colored_match_start - colored_pos);
895         dst += (colored_match_start - colored_pos);
896       }
897 
898       // Add highlight background
899       uint8_t r, g, b;
900       get_highlight_color(&r, &g, &b);
901       dst = append_truecolor_bg(dst, r, g, b);
902 
903       // Copy matched text, re-applying background after any [0m or [00m reset codes
904       size_t match_byte_len = colored_match_end - colored_match_start;
905       const char *match_src = colored_text + colored_match_start;
906       char *dst_end = highlight_buffer + sizeof(highlight_buffer) - 1; // Absolute buffer end
907       size_t i = 0;
908       while (i < match_byte_len) {
909         // Check for [0m or [00m reset codes
910         if (i + 4 <= match_byte_len && match_src[i] == '\x1b' && match_src[i + 1] == '[' && match_src[i + 2] == '0' &&
911             match_src[i + 3] == 'm') {
912           // Found [0m - copy it and re-apply background (if room)
913           if (dst + 4 >= dst_end)
914             break;                 // Not enough space for the reset code itself
915           *dst++ = match_src[i++]; // ESC
916           *dst++ = match_src[i++]; // '['
917           *dst++ = match_src[i++]; // '0'
918           *dst++ = match_src[i++]; // 'm'
919           // Re-apply highlight background after reset (only if room)
920           if (dst + 20 < dst_end) {
921             uint8_t r2, g2, b2;
922             get_highlight_color(&r2, &g2, &b2);
923             dst = append_truecolor_bg(dst, r2, g2, b2);
924           }
925         } else if (i + 5 <= match_byte_len && match_src[i] == '\x1b' && match_src[i + 1] == '[' &&
926                    match_src[i + 2] == '0' && match_src[i + 3] == '0' && match_src[i + 4] == 'm') {
927           // Found [00m - copy it and re-apply background (if room)
928           if (dst + 5 >= dst_end)
929             break;                 // Not enough space for the reset code itself
930           *dst++ = match_src[i++]; // ESC
931           *dst++ = match_src[i++]; // '['
932           *dst++ = match_src[i++]; // '0'
933           *dst++ = match_src[i++]; // '0'
934           *dst++ = match_src[i++]; // 'm'
935           // Re-apply highlight background after reset (only if room)
936           if (dst + 20 < dst_end) {
937             uint8_t r2, g2, b2;
938             get_highlight_color(&r2, &g2, &b2);
939             dst = append_truecolor_bg(dst, r2, g2, b2);
940           }
941         } else {
942           // Regular character - just copy
943           if (dst + 1 >= dst_end)
944             break;
945           *dst++ = match_src[i++];
946         }
947       }
948 
949       // Reset background only
950       memcpy(dst, "\x1b[49m", 5);
951       dst += 5;
952 
953       colored_pos = colored_match_end;
954       plain_offset = plain_match_end;
955 
956       // Prevent infinite loop on zero-length matches
957       if (plain_match_end == plain_match_start) {
958         plain_offset++;
959       }
960     }
961 
962     // Copy remaining text
963     if (colored_pos < colored_len) {
964       memcpy(dst, colored_text + colored_pos, colored_len - colored_pos);
965       dst += (colored_len - colored_pos);
966     }
967 
968     // Reset both background and foreground at end to prevent color bleeding
969     *dst = '\0';
970 
971     // Clean up match data if we created it for interactive grep
972     if (is_log_search && match_data && !global_pat) {
973       pcre2_match_data_free(match_data);
974     }
975 
976     return highlight_buffer;
977   }
978 
979   // Single match highlighting (same logic as global path)
980   // map_plain_to_colored_pos(N) returns byte position after counting N characters
981   size_t colored_start = map_plain_to_colored_pos(colored_text, match_start);
982   size_t colored_end = map_plain_to_colored_pos(colored_text, match_start + match_len);
983 
984   size_t colored_len = strlen(colored_text);
985   char *dst = highlight_buffer;
986 
987   // Copy everything before the match
988   if (colored_start > 0) {
989     memcpy(dst, colored_text, colored_start);
990     dst += colored_start;
991   }
992 
993   // Add highlight background
994   uint8_t r, g, b;
995   get_highlight_color(&r, &g, &b);
996   dst = append_truecolor_bg(dst, r, g, b);
997 
998   // Copy matched text, re-applying background after any [0m or [00m reset codes
999   size_t match_byte_len = colored_end - colored_start;
1000   const char *match_src = colored_text + colored_start;
1001   char *dst_end = highlight_buffer + sizeof(highlight_buffer) - 1; // Absolute buffer end
1002   size_t i = 0;
1003 
1004   while (i < match_byte_len) {
1005     // Check for [0m or [00m reset codes
1006     if (i + 4 <= match_byte_len && match_src[i] == '\x1b' && match_src[i + 1] == '[' && match_src[i + 2] == '0' &&
1007         match_src[i + 3] == 'm') {
1008       // Found [0m - copy it and re-apply background (if room)
1009       if (dst + 4 >= dst_end)
1010         break;                 // Not enough space for even the reset code
1011       *dst++ = match_src[i++]; // ESC
1012       *dst++ = match_src[i++]; // '['
1013       *dst++ = match_src[i++]; // '0'
1014       *dst++ = match_src[i++]; // 'm'
1015       // Re-apply highlight background after reset (only if room)
1016       if (dst + 20 < dst_end) {
1017         uint8_t r2, g2, b2;
1018         get_highlight_color(&r2, &g2, &b2);
1019         dst = append_truecolor_bg(dst, r2, g2, b2);
1020       }
1021     } else if (i + 5 <= match_byte_len && match_src[i] == '\x1b' && match_src[i + 1] == '[' &&
1022                match_src[i + 2] == '0' && match_src[i + 3] == '0' && match_src[i + 4] == 'm') {
1023       // Found [00m - copy it and re-apply background (if room)
1024       if (dst + 5 >= dst_end)
1025         break;                 // Not enough space for even the reset code
1026       *dst++ = match_src[i++]; // ESC
1027       *dst++ = match_src[i++]; // '['
1028       *dst++ = match_src[i++]; // '0'
1029       *dst++ = match_src[i++]; // '0'
1030       *dst++ = match_src[i++]; // 'm'
1031       // Re-apply highlight background after reset (only if room)
1032       if (dst + 20 < dst_end) {
1033         uint8_t r3, g3, b3;
1034         get_highlight_color(&r3, &g3, &b3);
1035         dst = append_truecolor_bg(dst, r3, g3, b3);
1036       }
1037     } else {
1038       // Regular character - just copy
1039       if (dst + 1 >= dst_end) {
1040         break;
1041       }
1042       *dst++ = match_src[i++];
1043     }
1044   }
1045 
1046   // Reset only the background color to preserve foreground colors from headers
1047   // Using \x1b[49m (reset background only) instead of \x1b[0m (reset all)
1048   memcpy(dst, "\x1b[49m", 5);
1049   dst += 5;
1050 
1051   // Copy remaining text
1052   size_t remaining = colored_len - colored_end;
1053   if (remaining > 0) {
1054     memcpy(dst, colored_text + colored_end, remaining);
1055     dst += remaining;
1056   }
1057 
1058   *dst = '\0';
1059   return highlight_buffer;
1060 }






References append_truecolor_bg(),
    asciichat_pcre2_singleton_get_code(),
    log_search_get_case_insensitive(),
    log_search_get_global_highlight(),
    log_search_get_input_buffer(), log_search_get_input_len(),
    log_search_get_pattern_singleton(), log_search_is_active(),
    SAFE_STRNCPY, grep_pattern_t::singleton, and
    utf8_strcasestr().


Referenced by grep_highlight_colored_copy(), and
    terminal_screen_render().






char
  * grep_highlight_colored_copy (const char * colored_text, const char *
  plain_text, size_t match_start, size_t match_len)


Highlight matches in colored text, returning a new allocated copy.
    Like grep_highlight_colored() but returns a newly allocated string
    instead of a static buffer. Caller must free the returned string with
    SAFE_FREE().




Parameters


colored_text Original text with ANSI color codes


plain_text Stripped plain text (for match positions)


match_start Match start position in plain text


match_len Match length in plain text


Returns


Newly allocated highlighted text (caller must free), or
  NULL on error


Definition at line 729 of file grep.c.


730                                                     {
731   const char *result = grep_highlight_colored(colored_text, plain_text, match_start, match_len);
732   if (result) {
733     size_t result_len = strlen(result) + 1;
734     char *copy = SAFE_MALLOC(result_len, char *);
735     if (copy) {
736       memcpy(copy, result, result_len);
737       return copy;
738     }
739   }
740   return NULL;
741 }






References grep_highlight_colored(), and
    SAFE_MALLOC.






asciichat_error_t grep_init (const char * pattern)


Initialize log filtering with PCRE2 regex pattern. Compiles the
    pattern eagerly at startup. If compilation fails, logs a warning and
    disables filtering.




Parameters


pattern PCRE2 regex pattern (NULL or empty = no
  filtering)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM on bad
  regex


Definition at line 486 of file grep.c.


486                                                  {
487   // Reject NULL or empty patterns
488   if (!pattern) {
489     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Pattern cannot be NULL");
490   }
491   if (strlen(pattern) == 0) {
492     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Pattern cannot be empty");
493   }
494 
495   // Parse pattern
496   parse_result_t parsed = grep_parse_pattern(pattern);
497   if (!parsed.valid) {
498     log_error("Invalid --grep pattern format: \"%s\"", pattern);
499     log_error("Use /pattern/flags format (e.g., \"/query/ig\") or plain regex (e.g., \"query\")");
500     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid --grep pattern format");
501   }
502 
503   // Allocate or expand pattern array
504   if (g_filter_state.pattern_count >= g_filter_state.pattern_capacity) {
505     int new_capacity = (g_filter_state.pattern_capacity == 0) ? 4 : g_filter_state.pattern_capacity * 2;
506     grep_pattern_t *new_patterns =
507         SAFE_REALLOC(g_filter_state.patterns, new_capacity * sizeof(grep_pattern_t), grep_pattern_t *);
508     if (!new_patterns) {
509       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate pattern array");
510     }
511     g_filter_state.patterns = new_patterns;
512     g_filter_state.pattern_capacity = new_capacity;
513   }
514 
515   // Add new pattern
516   grep_pattern_t *new_pat = &g_filter_state.patterns[g_filter_state.pattern_count];
517   memset(new_pat, 0, sizeof(*new_pat));
518 
519   // Make a copy of the original pattern string (argv pointers may be deallocated)
520   char *original_copy = SAFE_MALLOC(strlen(pattern) + 1, char *);
521   if (!original_copy) {
522     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate original pattern string");
523   }
524   memcpy(original_copy, pattern, strlen(pattern) + 1);
525   new_pat->original = original_copy;
526 
527   new_pat->parsed_pattern = SAFE_MALLOC(strlen(parsed.pattern) + 1, char *);
528   if (!new_pat->parsed_pattern) {
529     SAFE_FREE(original_copy);
530     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate pattern string");
531   }
532   memcpy(new_pat->parsed_pattern, parsed.pattern, strlen(parsed.pattern) + 1);
533 
534   new_pat->is_fixed_string = parsed.is_fixed_string;
535   new_pat->case_insensitive = parsed.case_insensitive;
536   new_pat->invert = parsed.invert;
537   new_pat->global_flag = parsed.global_flag;
538   new_pat->context_before = parsed.context_before;
539   new_pat->context_after = parsed.context_after;
540 
541   // Compile regex (skip for fixed strings)
542   if (!parsed.is_fixed_string) {
543     new_pat->singleton = asciichat_pcre2_singleton_compile(parsed.pattern, parsed.pcre2_options);
544     if (!new_pat->singleton) {
545       SAFE_FREE(new_pat->parsed_pattern);
546       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Failed to compile regex pattern");
547     }
548 
549     // Validate compilation
550     pcre2_code *code = asciichat_pcre2_singleton_get_code(new_pat->singleton);
551     if (!code) {
552       SAFE_FREE(new_pat->parsed_pattern);
553       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Regex compilation failed");
554     }
555   }
556 
557   g_filter_state.pattern_count++;
558 
559   // Update context buffer size if needed
560   if (parsed.context_before > g_filter_state.buffer_size) {
561     // Reallocate line buffer
562     char **new_buffer = SAFE_REALLOC(g_filter_state.line_buffer, parsed.context_before * sizeof(char *), char **);
563     if (!new_buffer) {
564       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate context line buffer");
565     }
566     // Initialize new slots
567     for (int i = g_filter_state.buffer_size; i < parsed.context_before; i++) {
568       new_buffer[i] = NULL;
569     }
570     g_filter_state.line_buffer = new_buffer;
571     g_filter_state.buffer_size = parsed.context_before;
572   }
573 
574   // Update max context_after
575   if (parsed.context_after > g_filter_state.max_context_after) {
576     g_filter_state.max_context_after = parsed.context_after;
577   }
578 
579   g_filter_state.enabled = true;
580   log_info("Added --grep pattern #%d: %s", g_filter_state.pattern_count, pattern);
581 
582   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
583 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    asciichat_pcre2_singleton_compile(),
    asciichat_pcre2_singleton_get_code(),
    grep_parse_result_t::case_insensitive,
    grep_pattern_t::case_insensitive,
    grep_parse_result_t::context_after,
    grep_pattern_t::context_after,
    grep_parse_result_t::context_before,
    grep_pattern_t::context_before, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ERROR_MEMORY, grep_parse_result_t::global_flag,
    grep_pattern_t::global_flag, grep_parse_pattern(),
    grep_parse_result_t::invert, grep_pattern_t::invert,
    grep_parse_result_t::is_fixed_string,
    grep_pattern_t::is_fixed_string, log_error, log_info,
    grep_pattern_t::original, grep_pattern_t::parsed_pattern,
    grep_parse_result_t::pattern,
    grep_parse_result_t::pcre2_options, SAFE_FREE,
    SAFE_MALLOC, SAFE_REALLOC, SET_ERRNO,
    grep_pattern_t::singleton, and grep_parse_result_t::valid.


Referenced by main().






grep_parse_result_t grep_parse_pattern (const char *
  input)


Parse pattern in /pattern/flags format or plain regex format.
    Supports two formats:


    
  
  • Format 1: /pattern/flags (e.g., "/error/igC2")
    • 
  
  • Format 2: plain pattern (e.g., "error")
    • 



Flags: i(case-insensitive), m(multiline), s(dotall), x(extended),
    g(global highlight), I(invert), F(fixed string), A<n>(after context),
    B<n>(before context), C<n>(both context)




Parameters


input Input pattern string


Returns


Parsed result with pattern, flags, and validity


Parse pattern in /pattern/flags format or plain regex format.




Parameters


input Input pattern string (either /pattern/flags,
  pattern/flags, or plain pattern)


Returns


Parse result with all extracted settings


Format 1 (with flags, explicit): /pattern/flags


    
  
  • Pattern enclosed in forward slashes with optional flags
    • 
  
  • Use F flag for fixed string (literal) matching
    • 



Format 2 (with flags, implicit): pattern/flags


    
  
  • Interactive grep format (no leading slash, since / starts the grep
      command)
    • 
  
  • Treats last slash as delimiter
    • 



Format 3 (plain regex): pattern


    
  
  • Plain regex pattern without slashes or flags
    • 
  
  • Treated as regex with default options (no flags)
    • 



Flags (formats 1 & 2):


    
  
  • i: case-insensitive
    • 
  
  • m: multiline mode (regex only)
    • 
  
  • s: dotall mode (regex only)
    • 
  
  • x: extended mode (regex only)
    • 
  
  • g: global (highlight all matches)
    • 
  
  • I: invert match (show non-matching lines)
    • 
  
  • F: fixed string (literal match, no regex)
    • 
  
  • A<n>: show n lines after match (e.g., A3)
    • 
  
  • B<n>: show n lines before match (e.g., B2)
    • 
  
  • C<n>: show n lines before and after match (e.g., C5)
    • 



Examples:


    
  
  • "/test/" - Simple regex with slashes
    • 
  
  • "test" - Plain regex without slashes
    • 
  
  • "/query/i" - Case-insensitive regex
    • 
  
  • "/test/F" - Fixed string match for "test"
    • 
  
  • "/api/v1/users/F" - Fixed string match for
      "api/v1/users"
    • 
  
  • "/ERROR/IA3" - Invert match + 3 lines after
    • 
  
  • "/FATAL/B2A5F" - Fixed string, 2 before, 5 after
    • 
  
  • "error|warn" - Plain regex with alternation
    • 
  
  • "search/i" - Interactive grep: case-insensitive
      "search"
    • 
  
  • "api/v1/users/F" - Interactive grep: fixed string match
    • 



Definition at line 351 of file grep.c.


351                                                           {
352   parse_result_t result = {0};
353   result.pcre2_options = PCRE2_UTF | PCRE2_UCP; // Default: UTF-8 mode
354 
355   if (!input || strlen(input) == 0) {
356     return result; // Invalid: empty pattern
357   }
358 
359   size_t len = strlen(input);
360   const char *closing_slash = NULL;
361   const char *pattern_start = input;
362 
363   // Check if pattern uses /pattern/flags format (explicit)
364   if (input[0] == '/') {
365     // Format 1: /pattern/flags
366     if (len < 3) {
367       return result; // Invalid: too short for /pattern/ format
368     }
369 
370     // Find closing slash - don't treat backslash as an escape character
371     // (backslashes are regex escapes, not delimiter escapes)
372     closing_slash = strchr(input + 1, '/');
373     if (!closing_slash) {
374       return result; // Invalid: missing closing /
375     }
376 
377     pattern_start = input + 1;
378   } else {
379     // Check for pattern/flags format (implicit, no leading slash)
380     // Find the last forward slash to use as delimiter
381     closing_slash = strrchr(input, '/');
382 
383     if (closing_slash && closing_slash > input) {
384       // There's at least one slash (not at the beginning)
385       // Try to parse as pattern/flags format
386       pattern_start = input;
387     } else {
388       // No slash or slash at beginning - treat as plain pattern
389       closing_slash = NULL;
390     }
391   }
392 
393   if (closing_slash) {
394     // Extract pattern between start and closing slash
395     size_t pattern_len = (size_t)(closing_slash - pattern_start);
396     if (pattern_len == 0) {
397       return result; // Invalid: empty pattern
398     }
399     if (pattern_len >= sizeof(result.pattern)) {
400       pattern_len = sizeof(result.pattern) - 1;
401     }
402     memcpy(result.pattern, pattern_start, pattern_len);
403     result.pattern[pattern_len] = '\0';
404 
405     // Parse flags after closing slash
406     // First pass: check for F flag
407     const char *flags = closing_slash + 1;
408     bool has_F_flag = (strchr(flags, 'F') != NULL);
409 
410     // Parse all flags
411     for (const char *p = flags; *p; p++) {
412       char c = *p;
413 
414       // Single-character flags
415       if (c == 'i') {
416         result.pcre2_options |= PCRE2_CASELESS;
417         result.case_insensitive = true;
418       } else if (c == 'm') {
419         result.pcre2_options |= PCRE2_MULTILINE;
420       } else if (c == 's') {
421         result.pcre2_options |= PCRE2_DOTALL;
422       } else if (c == 'x') {
423         result.pcre2_options |= PCRE2_EXTENDED;
424       } else if (c == 'g') {
425         result.global_flag = true;
426       } else if (c == 'I') {
427         result.invert = true;
428       } else if (c == 'F') {
429         result.is_fixed_string = true;
430       }
431       // Multi-character flags with integers
432       else if (c == 'A') {
433         p++; // Move to digits
434         int num = 0;
435         while (*p >= '0' && *p <= '9') {
436           num = num * 10 + (*p - '0');
437           p++;
438         }
439         p--;                                        // Back up one (for loop will increment)
440         result.context_after = (num > 0) ? num : 1; // Default to 1 if no number
441       } else if (c == 'B') {
442         p++;
443         int num = 0;
444         while (*p >= '0' && *p <= '9') {
445           num = num * 10 + (*p - '0');
446           p++;
447         }
448         p--;
449         result.context_before = (num > 0) ? num : 1;
450       } else if (c == 'C') {
451         p++;
452         int num = 0;
453         while (*p >= '0' && *p <= '9') {
454           num = num * 10 + (*p - '0');
455           p++;
456         }
457         p--;
458         int ctx = (num > 0) ? num : 1;
459         result.context_before = ctx;
460         result.context_after = ctx;
461       } else {
462         // Invalid flag character - only error if not using F flag
463         if (!has_F_flag) {
464           return result; // Invalid
465         }
466         // Otherwise ignore invalid flags (they're part of the fixed string context)
467       }
468     }
469   } else {
470     // Format 3: Plain pattern without slashes (treat as regex, no flags)
471     size_t pattern_len = len;
472     if (pattern_len >= sizeof(result.pattern)) {
473       pattern_len = sizeof(result.pattern) - 1;
474     }
475     memcpy(result.pattern, input, pattern_len);
476     result.pattern[pattern_len] = '\0';
477 
478     // No flags for plain format - just use default options
479     // (PCRE2_UTF | PCRE2_UCP already set at the top)
480   }
481 
482   result.valid = true;
483   return result;
484 }






References grep_parse_result_t::case_insensitive,
    grep_parse_result_t::context_after,
    grep_parse_result_t::context_before,
    grep_parse_result_t::global_flag, grep_parse_result_t::invert,
    grep_parse_result_t::is_fixed_string,
    grep_parse_result_t::pattern,
    grep_parse_result_t::pcre2_options, and
    grep_parse_result_t::valid.


Referenced by grep_init(), log_search_enter_mode(),
    log_search_exit_mode(), log_search_gather_and_filter_logs(),
    log_search_get_match_info(), log_search_handle_key(), and
    log_search_init().






asciichat_error_t grep_restore_patterns (void )


Restore previously saved filter patterns. Restores filter patterns
    saved by grep_save_patterns(). Used by interactive grep when user
    cancels (Escape) to restore CLI --grep patterns.




Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Definition at line 1253 of file grep.c.


1253                                               {
1254   // Clear current patterns (but don't free the array)
1255   for (int i = 0; i < g_filter_state.pattern_count; i++) {
1256     SAFE_FREE(g_filter_state.patterns[i].parsed_pattern);
1257     g_filter_state.patterns[i].singleton = NULL;
1258   }
1259 
1260   // Ensure we have capacity for saved patterns
1261   if (g_filter_state.saved_pattern_count > g_filter_state.pattern_capacity) {
1262     grep_pattern_t *new_patterns = SAFE_REALLOC(
1263         g_filter_state.patterns, g_filter_state.saved_pattern_count * sizeof(grep_pattern_t), grep_pattern_t *);
1264     if (!new_patterns) {
1265       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate pattern array for restore");
1266     }
1267     g_filter_state.patterns = new_patterns;
1268     g_filter_state.pattern_capacity = g_filter_state.saved_pattern_count;
1269   }
1270 
1271   // Restore patterns
1272   if (g_filter_state.saved_patterns) {
1273     for (int i = 0; i < g_filter_state.saved_pattern_count; i++) {
1274       grep_pattern_t *src = &g_filter_state.saved_patterns[i];
1275       grep_pattern_t *dst = &g_filter_state.patterns[i];
1276 
1277       // Copy pattern structure
1278       *dst = *src;
1279 
1280       // Deep copy parsed_pattern string
1281       dst->parsed_pattern = SAFE_MALLOC(strlen(src->parsed_pattern) + 1, char *);
1282       if (!dst->parsed_pattern) {
1283         // Clean up partial restore
1284         for (int j = 0; j < i; j++) {
1285           SAFE_FREE(g_filter_state.patterns[j].parsed_pattern);
1286         }
1287         g_filter_state.pattern_count = 0;
1288         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to restore pattern string");
1289       }
1290       memcpy(dst->parsed_pattern, src->parsed_pattern, strlen(src->parsed_pattern) + 1);
1291 
1292       // Singleton pointers are shared (reference counted)
1293       dst->singleton = src->singleton;
1294     }
1295   }
1296 
1297   g_filter_state.pattern_count = g_filter_state.saved_pattern_count;
1298   g_filter_state.enabled = g_filter_state.saved_enabled;
1299 
1300   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1301 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, ERROR_MEMORY,
    grep_pattern_t::parsed_pattern, SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC,
    SAFE_REALLOC, SET_ERRNO, and
  grep_pattern_t::singleton.


Referenced by log_search_exit_mode().






asciichat_error_t grep_save_patterns (void )


Save current filter patterns for later restoration. Saves the
    current state of CLI --grep patterns so they can be restored later. Used by
    interactive grep to preserve initial filters when entering interactive
  mode.




Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Definition at line 1203 of file grep.c.


1203                                            {
1204   // Free previous saved patterns if any
1205   if (g_filter_state.saved_patterns) {
1206     for (int i = 0; i < g_filter_state.saved_pattern_count; i++) {
1207       SAFE_FREE(g_filter_state.saved_patterns[i].parsed_pattern);
1208     }
1209     SAFE_FREE(g_filter_state.saved_patterns);
1210   }
1211 
1212   // Allocate saved pattern array
1213   if (g_filter_state.pattern_count > 0) {
1214     g_filter_state.saved_patterns =
1215         SAFE_MALLOC(g_filter_state.pattern_count * sizeof(grep_pattern_t), grep_pattern_t *);
1216     if (!g_filter_state.saved_patterns) {
1217       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate saved pattern array");
1218     }
1219 
1220     // Deep copy all patterns
1221     for (int i = 0; i < g_filter_state.pattern_count; i++) {
1222       grep_pattern_t *src = &g_filter_state.patterns[i];
1223       grep_pattern_t *dst = &g_filter_state.saved_patterns[i];
1224 
1225       // Copy pattern structure
1226       *dst = *src;
1227 
1228       // Deep copy parsed_pattern string
1229       dst->parsed_pattern = SAFE_MALLOC(strlen(src->parsed_pattern) + 1, char *);
1230       if (!dst->parsed_pattern) {
1231         // Clean up partial save
1232         for (int j = 0; j < i; j++) {
1233           SAFE_FREE(g_filter_state.saved_patterns[j].parsed_pattern);
1234         }
1235         SAFE_FREE(g_filter_state.saved_patterns);
1236         g_filter_state.saved_patterns = NULL;
1237         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to save pattern string");
1238       }
1239       memcpy(dst->parsed_pattern, src->parsed_pattern, strlen(src->parsed_pattern) + 1);
1240 
1241       // Singleton pointers are shared (reference counted)
1242       dst->singleton = src->singleton;
1243     }
1244   }
1245 
1246   g_filter_state.saved_pattern_count = g_filter_state.pattern_count;
1247   g_filter_state.saved_pattern_capacity = g_filter_state.pattern_count;
1248   g_filter_state.saved_enabled = g_filter_state.enabled;
1249 
1250   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1251 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, ERROR_MEMORY,
    grep_pattern_t::parsed_pattern, SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC,
    SET_ERRNO, and grep_pattern_t::singleton.


Referenced by log_search_enter_mode().






bool grep_should_output (const char * log_line, size_t *
  match_start, size_t * match_len)


Check if log line should be output (matches filter). Thread-safe:
    Uses thread-local match_data storage. Non-allocating: Reuses pre-allocated
    match_data.




Parameters


log_line Formatted log line to check


match_start Output: start offset of match


match_len Output: length of match


Returns


true if line should be output, false to suppress


Definition at line 585 of file grep.c.


585                                                                                       {
586   // Reject NULL lines
587   if (!log_line) {
588     if (match_start)
589       *match_start = 0;
590     if (match_len)
591       *match_len = 0;
592     return false;
593   }
594 
595   // If filtering disabled, output everything
596   if (!g_filter_state.enabled) {
597     return true;
598   }
599 
600   // Check if we're in "context after" mode (outputting lines after a match)
601   if (g_filter_state.lines_after_match > 0) {
602     g_filter_state.lines_after_match--;
603     *match_start = 0;
604     *match_len = 0;
605 
606     // Store in buffer even during context-after mode
607     if (g_filter_state.buffer_size > 0) {
608       SAFE_FREE(g_filter_state.line_buffer[g_filter_state.buffer_pos]);
609       size_t line_len = strlen(log_line) + 1;
610       g_filter_state.line_buffer[g_filter_state.buffer_pos] = SAFE_MALLOC(line_len, char *);
611       if (g_filter_state.line_buffer[g_filter_state.buffer_pos]) {
612         memcpy(g_filter_state.line_buffer[g_filter_state.buffer_pos], log_line, line_len);
613       }
614       g_filter_state.buffer_pos = (g_filter_state.buffer_pos + 1) % g_filter_state.buffer_size;
615     }
616 
617     return true; // Output context line
618   }
619 
620   pcre2_match_data *match_data = get_thread_match_data();
621   size_t line_len = strlen(log_line);
622 
623   // Try each pattern (OR logic)
624   bool any_match = false;
625   grep_pattern_t *matched_pattern = NULL;
626 
627   for (int i = 0; i < g_filter_state.pattern_count; i++) {
628     grep_pattern_t *pat = &g_filter_state.patterns[i];
629     bool this_match = false;
630 
631     if (pat->is_fixed_string) {
632       // Fixed string matching (with optional case-insensitive support)
633       const char *found;
634       if (pat->case_insensitive) {
635         // Unicode-aware case-insensitive search
636         found = utf8_strcasestr(log_line, pat->parsed_pattern);
637       } else {
638         // Case-sensitive search
639         found = strstr(log_line, pat->parsed_pattern);
640       }
641 
642       if (found) {
643         this_match = true;
644         if (!matched_pattern) {
645           *match_start = (size_t)(found - log_line);
646           *match_len = strlen(pat->parsed_pattern);
647           matched_pattern = pat;
648         }
649       }
650     } else {
651       // Regex matching
652       if (!match_data || !pat->singleton) {
653         continue;
654       }
655 
656       pcre2_code *code = asciichat_pcre2_singleton_get_code(pat->singleton);
657       if (!code) {
658         continue;
659       }
660 
661       int rc = pcre2_jit_match(code, (PCRE2_SPTR)log_line, line_len, 0, 0, match_data, NULL);
662       if (rc >= 0) {
663         this_match = true;
664         if (!matched_pattern) {
665           PCRE2_SIZE *ovector = pcre2_get_ovector_pointer(match_data);
666           *match_start = (size_t)ovector[0];
667           *match_len = (size_t)(ovector[1] - ovector[0]);
668           matched_pattern = pat;
669         }
670       }
671     }
672 
673     // Apply invert flag
674     if (pat->invert) {
675       this_match = !this_match;
676     }
677 
678     if (this_match) {
679       any_match = true;
680       // Update matched_pattern if this one has more context
681       if (!matched_pattern || (pat->context_before + pat->context_after) >
682                                   (matched_pattern->context_before + matched_pattern->context_after)) {
683         matched_pattern = pat;
684       }
685     }
686   }
687 
688   // If any pattern matched
689   if (any_match && matched_pattern) {
690     // Output context_before lines from circular buffer
691     if (matched_pattern->context_before > 0 && g_filter_state.buffer_size > 0) {
692       int num_lines = (matched_pattern->context_before < g_filter_state.buffer_size) ? matched_pattern->context_before
693                                                                                      : g_filter_state.buffer_size;
694 
695       // Calculate starting position in circular buffer
696       int start_pos = (g_filter_state.buffer_pos - num_lines + g_filter_state.buffer_size) % g_filter_state.buffer_size;
697 
698       // Output buffered lines
699       for (int i = 0; i < num_lines; i++) {
700         int pos = (start_pos + i) % g_filter_state.buffer_size;
701         if (g_filter_state.line_buffer[pos]) {
702           fprintf(stderr, "%s\n", g_filter_state.line_buffer[pos]);
703         }
704       }
705     }
706 
707     // Set up context_after counter
708     if (matched_pattern->context_after > 0) {
709       g_filter_state.lines_after_match = matched_pattern->context_after;
710     }
711 
712     return true; // Match found, output line
713   }
714 
715   // No match - store in context buffer for potential future use
716   if (g_filter_state.buffer_size > 0) {
717     SAFE_FREE(g_filter_state.line_buffer[g_filter_state.buffer_pos]);
718     size_t line_len_copy = strlen(log_line) + 1;
719     g_filter_state.line_buffer[g_filter_state.buffer_pos] = SAFE_MALLOC(line_len_copy, char *);
720     if (g_filter_state.line_buffer[g_filter_state.buffer_pos]) {
721       memcpy(g_filter_state.line_buffer[g_filter_state.buffer_pos], log_line, line_len_copy);
722     }
723     g_filter_state.buffer_pos = (g_filter_state.buffer_pos + 1) % g_filter_state.buffer_size;
724   }
725 
726   return false; // No match, suppress line
727 }






References asciichat_pcre2_singleton_get_code(),
    grep_pattern_t::case_insensitive,
    grep_pattern_t::context_after, grep_pattern_t::context_before,
    grep_pattern_t::invert, grep_pattern_t::is_fixed_string,
    grep_pattern_t::parsed_pattern, SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC,
    grep_pattern_t::singleton, and utf8_strcasestr().








Author


Zachary Fogg <me@zfo.gg>







  

    Version 0.9.19
    ascii-chat
